data.body:
PASS-1 review — rubric T11 (Data-migration / backfill safety, overlay): NOT-APPLICABLE / PASS — no findings.

Reviewing the FOLLOW-ON plan "Reinstate per-pass latency/cost telemetry (coverage.metrics) on the workflow plan-review gate" (re-add det_ms/llm_ms/total_ms/llm_calls/claim_path that B-RETIRE dropped with run_review). T11 is an overlay (migration-EXECUTION safety) that engages ONLY when a plan changes a persisted schema/format IN-PLACE or BACKFILLS existing data. This plan does neither.

Grounded reasoning (plan text + established rebar event model):

- ADDITIVE, FORWARD-ONLY CAPTURE: The plan reconstructs coverage['metrics'] at RUN TIME from the workflow recorder's per-step duration_ms ("gate_dispatch.produce_plan_review_verdict reconstructs coverage['metrics'] from rec.steps after the run, attaching it to the verdict") and carries it on the already-existing reducer-ignored REVIEW_RESULT sidecar. This is an in-memory dict assembly emitted on NEW reviews going forward — not a rewrite of any existing persisted record. T11(a) expand-contract / add-nullable→backfill→enforce / blocking-DDL has no surface.

- NO BACKFILL OF HISTORICAL DATA: The plan re-instruments the live workflow run to PRODUCE metrics on new reviews; it does not read prior REVIEW_RESULT sidecars and repopulate them. Sidecars emitted during the regression window simply lack metrics and are left untouched (coverage is a free-form object, additionalProperties: true). So T11(b) batching/throttling, (c) resumability/idempotent re-run, (d) dual-write window, and (e) partial-failure rollback have nothing to apply to.

- NO BLOCKING DDL / NO LARGE-TABLE LOCK: rebar's store is an append-only, file-per-event git log, not a relational table — no DDL, no table lock, no single-shot blocking migration. The T11 MAJOR trigger (irreversible single-shot migration / long blocking lock on a large table) has no surface here.

- The plan's own tolerance clause ("a missing duration contributes 0 so it never raises inside the gate"; attached on BOTH the happy path and the coach-failure recovery path) is per-run robustness, not a migration-resumability concern — it does not pull T11 into scope.

ANTI-FP: T11 is explicitly not-applicable for non-persisted/in-memory changes and additive/non-rewriting persistence with no backfill.

Affirmations (T11): The plan's own text affirmatively establishes a purely additive, forward-capture-only instrumentation change (reconstruct metrics from recorder step timings; emit on the workflow path; carry on the existing append-only sidecar; test produced offline) with no persisted-format rewrite, no blocking DDL, and no backfill of historical sidecars. Under the overlay gate + ANTI-FP clause, T11 is not-applicable — clean, no findings.
